worth_it_explanation: Amazon owned-and-operated inventory is a fraction of a typical Amazon DSP campaign's total impressions. Third-party exchanges supply the rest, and the default state for exchange access is broad. An unconfigured line item sends spend to every connected exchange, including sources where low-quality and made-for-advertising (MFA) domains concentrate. These checks confirm that supply sources are selected with intent, that known low-quality domains are excluded, and that the supply path from publisher to impression is direct enough to be worth the cost.
intro: Amazon DSP buys inventory across Amazon's owned-and-operated properties, including Amazon.com, Prime Video, IMDb, Twitch, and Fire TV, and also through third-party exchanges. The third-party supply layer reaches a large audience but introduces quality variation. Line items that treat all exchanges and all supply sources as equivalent send spend to placements that offer low engagement, unsafe adjacency, or reseller-padded paths where the cost of the impression does not reflect the cost of the media. These checks confirm that supply is targeted deliberately, that exclusions are in place to block low-quality inventory, and that the supply path is examined for routes worth deprioritizing.

audit_checks.title: Third-party supply sources are not curated and every exchange receives spend by default
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Spend distributed across every connected exchange without intentional selection reaches low-quality placements that produce low engagement and inflate impression counts without delivering real attention. The budget assigned to those placements is not recoverable once it runs.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP line items include supply source settings that control which exchanges and supply types are eligible to serve the line item's ads. The default configuration leaves most sources enabled, which means spend flows to every connected third-party exchange unless an advertiser narrows the selection.

Third-party exchanges vary in the quality of inventory they aggregate. Some exchanges carry a high concentration of placements on made-for-advertising (MFA) sites: properties built primarily to generate ad revenue rather than serve a reader, often featuring republished or thin content, aggressive ad density, and traffic that includes a material share of invalid visits. Spend on those placements generates impressions that look real in the delivery report but do not represent a meaningful audience encounter with the ad.

Amazon Publisher Direct is a distinct supply path that connects Amazon DSP directly to publishers who have agreed to Amazon's standards for quality and brand suitability. Prioritizing Amazon Publisher Direct supply and selecting exchanges known for quality inventory, rather than leaving every exchange active, reduces the share of spend that reaches lower-quality placements.

A few signs point to uncurated supply on a line item:
  • Supply source settings show all exchanges enabled with no custom selection applied
  • The delivery report shows a long tail of low-visibility domains in the placement performance view
  • Average viewability falls below what the line item's audience and format would suggest
You may want to review the supply source settings on active line items and confirm that the selection reflects a deliberate choice rather than the default.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser driving to a product detail page, low-quality placements reduce the likelihood of reaching a shopper in a context where the ad can influence a purchase decision. A more curated supply list concentrates spend on placements where the audience is closer to a buying mindset.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser using the Amazon Ad Tag pixel to attribute off-Amazon conversions, low-quality placements inflate impression delivery without contributing conversions. Narrowing supply sources reduces wasted impressions and makes the conversion rate a more reliable signal.

investigate.intro: Review the supply source configuration on active line items and check the placement performance report for signs of low-quality inventory receiving spend.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the order you want to review and select a line item.
Navigate to the line item's inventory settings and locate the supply sources or exchange selection section.
Note whether all exchanges are enabled, whether Amazon Publisher Direct is prioritized, and whether any custom supply selection has been applied.

Open the line item's delivery report and filter to the placement or domain breakdown. Look for a long tail of unfamiliar domains with low viewability or click-through rates relative to the rest of the delivery.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, whether supply sources have been intentionally configured or left at the default all-enabled state. A line item with a deliberate supply source selection and no unexpected domain tail in the placement report passes. A line item with all exchanges active and a visible tail of low-quality placements is a fail recorded with the line item name and the order it belongs to.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the supply source configuration and any placement report observations for each line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that third-party supply sources on {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP line items have not been curated, leaving all connected exchanges active by default. Spend is reaching a broad mix of placements that includes low-quality inventory where impressions do not represent real audience engagement. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the supply source settings on each active line item, prioritizing Amazon Publisher Direct, and narrowing exchange selection to sources with demonstrated quality rather than accepting the default all-enabled configuration. After the change, pulling a placement performance report to confirm the domain tail has narrowed is a useful way to verify the adjustment took effect.

audit_checks.title: Made-for-advertising and low-quality domains are not excluded from delivery
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Made-for-advertising sites are built to harvest ad spend rather than serve readers, and they typically mix valid-looking impressions with a meaningful share of invalid traffic. Spend that reaches them pays for placements with no real audience value, and the invalid activity within them can distort performance metrics used to evaluate the campaign.

educate.base:
Made-for-advertising (MFA) sites are properties constructed primarily to attract programmatic ad spend. They rely on aggressive ad density, minimal original content, and high-volume traffic acquisition rather than organic readership. The IAB Tech Lab defines MFA properties in its Authorized Digital Sellers (ads.txt) and sellers.json frameworks as part of broader supply chain transparency efforts, but MFA sites can still participate in exchanges while appearing to pass standard quality checks.

Amazon DSP allows advertisers to maintain domain exclusion lists at the advertiser or order level. An exclusion list blocks specific domains from serving ads regardless of which line items are active under the order. Without an explicit exclusion list, spend can reach domains that have been publicly identified as MFA by industry bodies or flagged internally by verification vendors.

A few signs point to an absence of domain exclusions:
  • No domain exclusion list is attached at the advertiser or order level
  • The placement performance report includes domains that appear in published MFA or low-quality site lists
  • A third-party verification vendor's invalid traffic (IVT) report shows elevated IVT rates on specific domains
You may want to review whether a domain exclusion list is in place and whether it reflects current guidance from industry sources or the advertiser's verification vendor.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser, ad spend on MFA placements pays for impressions that do not influence purchase decisions. The reported impression volume holds steady while the share of spend reaching real shoppers falls.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser, MFA placements inflate the impression count without contributing to the Amazon Ad Tag pixel's conversion signal. Attribution data built on inflated impression counts can misrepresent which placements are earning conversions.

learn_more.source_title: Authorized Digital Sellers — ads.txt and app-ads.txt

investigate.intro: Confirm whether a domain exclusion list is attached at the advertiser or order level and check the placement performance report for domains appearing on published MFA or low-quality site lists.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the advertiser settings and locate the brand safety or inventory exclusion section. Note whether a domain exclusion list exists and when it was last updated.
Open a representative line item's placement performance report and download the domain breakdown.
Cross-reference the domains in the report against a published list of MFA or low-quality properties from a verification vendor or an industry source. Note any matches.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether a domain exclusion list is in place at the advertiser or order level, and whether the placement performance report includes domains from published MFA or low-quality lists. An account with an active, recently updated exclusion list and no flagged domains in the placement report passes. An account with no exclusion list, or one where the placement report includes known MFA domains, is a fail recorded with the specific domains identified and the line item or order where they appear.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the exclusion list status and any flagged domains from the placement report above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP account does not have an active domain exclusion list to block made-for-advertising and low-quality placements. Without explicit exclusions, spend reaches properties that generate impressions without real audience engagement, and the invalid traffic that concentrates on those properties can distort performance metrics. {{voice_recommend}} building a domain exclusion list at the advertiser or order level using guidance from a verification vendor or published industry sources, attaching it to active orders, and reviewing the placement performance report after it takes effect to confirm the flagged domains have been removed from delivery.

audit_checks.title: Supply path has not been reviewed for reseller-heavy or duplicative routes
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When the same impression reaches the bidder through multiple intermediaries rather than directly from the publisher, each intermediary takes a margin, and the effective cost per impression rises without improving the quality of the placement. Spend on reseller-heavy paths pays more for the same media than direct paths would require.

educate.base:
The OpenRTB SupplyChain object and sellers.json standard, published by the IAB Tech Lab, lets buyers see how many intermediaries sit between a publisher and the exchange presenting the impression for bid. Each node in the chain represents an entity that takes a revenue share, so a bid that flows through three intermediaries before reaching a buyer pays more per net impression dollar than a bid that flows directly from a publisher's authorized seller.

Supply path optimization (SPO) is the practice of identifying and deprioritizing reseller-heavy routes in favor of shorter, more direct paths to the publisher. Amazon DSP's deal management tools allow advertisers to establish direct or near-direct deals through private marketplace (PMP) arrangements with publishers who are part of Amazon Publisher Direct, which bypasses much of the open-exchange intermediary chain.

Without reviewing supply path data, spend runs through the full mix of direct and reseller paths, and the effective media cost is higher than it would be on a curated set of shorter paths.

A few signs point to an unreviewed supply path:
  • No private marketplace deals are active alongside open-exchange buys on the same audience
  • Supply chain data from a verification vendor shows a high proportion of impressions arriving through more than two hops
  • Amazon Publisher Direct supply is not prioritized in the line item's supply source settings
You may want to consider whether the campaign is structured to take advantage of direct supply relationships where they are available.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser, direct publisher relationships through Amazon Publisher Direct offer access to premium contextual placements alongside the audience targeting Amazon DSP provides. A shorter supply path on those placements reduces the cost per impression without changing the audience.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser focused on reach and awareness, the cost savings from shorter supply paths can be redirected toward additional impression volume on quality placements rather than paying intermediary margins.

investigate.intro: Review whether Amazon Publisher Direct supply is prioritized and whether any private marketplace deals are active. If a verification vendor is in use, check the supply chain data for impression-level path length.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the line item's supply source settings and note whether Amazon Publisher Direct is selected or prioritized alongside open-exchange sources.

Navigate to the deals section under the order or advertiser and note any active private marketplace deals. If none exist, note whether the campaign objectives would benefit from direct publisher relationships.
If a third-party measurement vendor is integrated, check the supply chain report for the average number of intermediary hops on delivered impressions.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether Amazon Publisher Direct supply is prioritized in the line item settings, whether any private marketplace deals are active, and, where a verification vendor is available, what the supply chain hop data shows. A campaign with Amazon Publisher Direct prioritized and at least one direct deal active on premium placements passes. A campaign with no supply path review, no direct deals, and no Amazon Publisher Direct prioritization is a fail recorded with the order name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the supply source settings, any active deals, and any available supply chain data for each order above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaigns have not been reviewed for supply path efficiency. Open-exchange impressions arriving through multiple reseller hops cost more per net impression than direct or near-direct paths, and the difference accrues across every impression the campaign delivers. {{voice_recommend}} prioritizing Amazon Publisher Direct in the line item supply source settings, exploring private marketplace deals for high-priority placements, and, where a verification vendor is available, reviewing supply chain data to identify and deprioritize routes with more than two intermediary hops. Redirecting spend toward shorter supply paths lowers the effective cost per impression without changing the audience targeting.
